A curated roundup of yesterday's top tech stories (2026-04-22).

## 1. Meta to track employee activity on computers to train its AI agents: Report - Business Standard

Meta plans to monitor employee activity on computers to train its AI agents. This information is being gathered for the purpose of training its artificial intelligence systems. The source of this information is a report published by Business Standard. The article details the methodology and implications of this internal data collection process. This indicates a focus on using real-time operational data to improve the functionality of Meta's AI. This development points to an internal focus on leveraging employee digital footprints for advanced AI development.

**Source:** Business Standard — [Read original](https://news.google.?oc=5)

## 2. Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models - TechCrunch

Meta plans to record employee keystrokes to train its artificial intelligence models, according to a report by TechCrunch.

This involves capturing the input data generated by employees as they type on their keyboards. This data will then be utilized to train the company's AI models. The article suggests this is part of Meta's process for developing its AI systems.

This development raises questions about workplace monitoring and privacy within large technology companies. The implications touch upon employee surveillance and data ethics in the context of artificial intelligence training.

**Source:** TechCrunch — [Read original](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiqgFBVV95cUxQWDRzWnRIZjRLUFJQdWNmSWZmOWpLV2pqSnBBSVA4RUNpV1RLZXpYam9pNWo4dzA3elpnYmhZbUdrRGVUN2ZYb3RCOGFMb0VfY0ZQdWJKNHJkSnMzSXBTMTh3REVXOEMzU0NvUjdDUGhEQlRFMVQzM3ZDcmhFUERqanBCbjZPS1FUc25mYnA3dFBWRTNEYUVfakM3SXZ1alRzUExiZ2VZbWVmQQ?oc=5)

## 3. Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models - El.kz

Meta plans to record employee keystrokes to train its artificial intelligence models. This action suggests an internal effort by Meta to use user interaction data for machine learning purposes. The keystroke data collected is likely intended to improve AI systems related to their platforms or internal operations. This practice raises questions regarding employee privacy and data handling policies within the company. This development highlights the increasing use of internal data for advanced AI training in the technology sector.

**Source:** El.kz — [Read original](https://news.google.?oc=5)

## 4. Meta begins collecting employee mouse movements and keystrokes as AI training data. - GIGAZINE

Meta is starting to collect employee mouse movements and keystrokes to use as data for training its artificial intelligence models. This data collection is being done in the context of training AI. The source of this development is GIGAZINE.

The article indicates that Meta is implementing a practice of capturing specific user input data from its employees. This data includes information about how employees move their mice and the keys they press on the keyboard. This information is being gathered with the explicit purpose of using it for training their AI systems.

This development raises questions about employee privacy and monitoring within the workplace. The broader impact relates to the evolving ethical considerations surrounding the use of employee activity data in AI development.

**Source:** GIGAZINE — [Read original](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiiwFBVV95cUxNcEhoRzJiZjBCd1RyNEJMeEI1X2RwNUxONUswMDh4OTVpbTA0aHRnNnI4WENzanA2bklfaWw0YzRRUVlzV2JFdkkzM3ppakE3M3drVEF2LTQ4UlVGSHlQTUpXdUNVTWw1QUM1YlNuMGRmVlJtRDdoTHktVjRWemNQbGtsc09oNXZCUGcw?oc=5)
